claims.claim: We present Chain-of-Density (CoD) evidence packing, an offline method that compresses retrieved evidence into token-budgeted prompt packs by iteratively extracting high-density support sentences and replacing lower-density spans.

evidence_refs.quote: # Chain-of-Density Evidence Packing for Budgeted Retrieval-Augmented Prompts

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: In a synthetic benchmark spanning five task types with query perturbations, CoD packing achieved a mean same-budget F1-proxy gain of 0.796 and mean gold-evidence recall gain of 0.743 over vanilla retrieve-then-read, with mean token savings of 0.491 against non-trivial vanilla baselines and a construction latency overhead of 1.191 ms.

evidence_refs.quote: # Chain-of-Density Evidence Packing for Budgeted Retrieval-Augmented Prompts

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: On a real-data validation using 120 SQuAD v1.1 dev examples with hard lexical distractors, CoD packing gained +0.105 answer EM/F1 and +0.098 faithful citation rate at matched token budgets, at a cost of +2.19 ms construction latency.

evidence_refs.quote: # Chain-of-Density Evidence Packing for Budgeted Retrieval-Augmented Prompts

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: A notable mixed finding is that CoD consumed more prompt tokens than vanilla on average in the real-data setting, because the vanilla baseline silently skipped oversized raw paragraphs; the stronger supported claim is same-budget quality gain rather than token reduction.

claims.claim: Chain-of-Density (CoD) evidence packing addresses this by rewriting the evidence selection step: rather than including or excluding whole chunks, the packer extracts and scores individual support sentences, then iteratively replaces lower-density spans with higher-density alternatives until the token budget is saturated.
